title: Gallatin Performed Its Daily Ballet Of Brakes, Cones, And Quiet Screaming
deck: A prototype traffic quick hit for the people who said they were leaving in five and are still looking at the same vape shop sign.
body: The morning crawl on Gallatin is exactly the kind of resident pain East Meets Nash should track: useful, local, and emotionally expensive. Launch coverage should pair traffic notes with construction records, NDOT alerts, and resident sightings.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_17
comments.text: If the cones start charging rent, they can afford the new apartments.

social.x: Gallatin is once again less of a road and more of a group project with brake lights.
social.instagram: The daily Gallatin crawl: cones, vibes, and everyone pretending this route was a choice.
social.video: Parker stands at a crosswalk with coffee and declares the traffic cone East Nashville's most stable resident.

title: A New Restaurant Soft-Launched So Softly Half The Neighborhood Is Pretending They Already Knew
deck: The launch playbook: one blurry Instagram story, three excited texts, and a reservation list that somehow has hierarchy.
body: This is the kind of restaurant/opening format the site should run constantly: what opened, where, who posted first, what the menu hints at, and whether it belongs in the daily brief or weekly issue.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_04
comments.text: I need to know if the fries are real fries or architectural potatoes.

social.x: A new Five Points spot appears to be in the soft-launch fog machine phase.
social.instagram: New place, soft launch, everyone suddenly an investor in the comment section.
social.video: Mabel reviews the sacred stages of an East Nashville restaurant opening: rumor, blurry photo, patio judgment.

title: The Sidewalk Parent Index Is Flashing Red Around Shelby
deck: Stroller math, scooter dodgeball, and one crosswalk that appears to be decorative.
body: A recurring family/civic column can track walkability, kids' activities, playground conditions, and the specific places where normal family logistics become endurance sport.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_22
comments.text: Please add a sub-index for playground shade and snack distance.

social.x: Launching soon: the Sidewalk Parent Index, because some crosswalks feel theoretical.
social.instagram: Shelby-area parent logistics, ranked by coffee loss and stroller vibration.
social.video: Parker attempts to narrate a peaceful walk and immediately finds three civic side quests.

title: The Permit File Is Whispering Again
deck: When the boring PDF starts explaining why everyone is suddenly yelling about parking.
body: June Parcel's watchdog lane should convert permits, agendas, zoning applications, and council records into readable, funny, useful context for residents.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_09
comments.text: June reading permit PDFs so I can remain emotionally unqualified is the service.

social.x: A permit file is not boring if it explains the fight everyone is about to have.
social.instagram: June found the boring document. Unfortunately it matters.
social.video: June opens a permit file like a detective opening a cold case.

title: The Weekend Calendar Is Already Acting Like It Has A Publicist
deck: Markets, shows, pop-ups, patio decisions, and the eternal question of whether you should leave the house.
body: Events coverage should be lightweight but high-volume: calendars, picks, social captions, ticket links, and a social-first version for each event cluster.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_31
comments.text: Please rank by parking pain. I am old now.

social.x: The weekend calendar is doing a lot, emotionally and logistically.
social.instagram: Your weekend options, sorted by vibe, distance, and how much you enjoy standing near a food truck.
social.video: Lila runs through the weekend like a weather report for extroverts.

title: What People Are Mad About This Week: Parking, Patios, And A Suspiciously Confident Sign
deck: A recurring outrage forecast for East Nashville's most renewable natural resource: informed irritation.
body: This should become a signature module. It can synthesize Facebook, Nextdoor, comments, tips, and public records into a funny but sourced neighborhood mood board.
label: Group Chat Says

comments.author: sample_neighbor_02
comments.text: The sign knows what it did.

social.x: This week's neighborhood outrage forecast includes parking, patios, and one sign with too much confidence.
social.instagram: What people are mad about this week, presented with love and a small amount of fear.
social.video: Mabel reads the outrage forecast like severe weather.

title: Riff Investigates Whether A New Venue Has Soul Or Just Edison Bulbs
deck: A nightlife review format for the city where every room claims it is intimate and every tab says otherwise.
body: Nightlife coverage should mix listings, reviews, owner posts, community chatter, ticket links, and Riff's recurring suspicion of anything with a mural too eager to be photographed.
label: Seed Item

comments.author: sample_neighbor_15
comments.text: Does the sound person look tired? That is my quality metric.

social.x: Riff's first venue test: does the room have soul, or just a lighting package?
social.instagram: East side venue energy, judged by sound, crowd, and mural desperation.
social.video: Riff stands outside a venue and describes Edison bulbs as a cry for help.

title: Classifieds Are Coming For Jobs, Gear, Kid Stuff, And Neighborhood Pleas
deck: A self-serve marketplace where East Nashville can sell a guitar pedal, find a babysitter, or beg someone to take the extra moving boxes.
body: Classifieds are an early ATXP-commerce fit: charge a small amount, let logged-in users post, and make the listings feel local enough to beat generic marketplaces.
label: Confirmed

comments.author: sample_neighbor_44
comments.text: Please add a category for toys my kid ignored immediately.

social.x: East Nashville classifieds should include jobs, gear, kid stuff, and public pleas about cardboard.
social.instagram: Coming soon: classifieds for the exact radius where your weird extra chair might find love.
social.video: Lila pitches classifieds like QVC for neighborhood odds and ends.
